title: acaena ovalifolia
intro: Acaena ovalifolia, also known as woolly-peg or small-leaf woolly-head, is a flowering plant species in the rose family that is native to New Zealand and southeastern Australia. The leaves and stems of Acaena ovalifolia contain saponins, which have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Acaena ovalifolia include reducing inflammation and relieving pain, as well as its potential use as an antibacterial agent to treat minor wounds and skin infections. In herbal preparations, Acaena ovalifolia can be used as a tincture, infusion, or ointment, with its saponins being extracted and used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
